items.title: Britain  isnt ready for war  and would be vulnerable to an attack from Vladimir Putin

For the first time, Ukraine is developing its own ballistic missiles and deep-strike capabilities. These are not merely systems under development, but real capabilities that are already operational and reaching distances that were previously unattainable. 2,884 kilometers. That is the distance from here to the Omsk oil refinery. A historic range. Historic precision. And unlike Russia, which wages war against residential buildings, schools, and churches, Ukraine strikes the “sponsors” of Putin’s war and the tools that help him kill. Some say that, with modern technology, we are sending Russia back to the Middle Ages. Others recall Napoleon and the days when Moscow was ablaze. But for Ukraine, it is not grand rhetoric that matters – it is the result. Our ultimate goal is not Russia without gasoline, but Ukraine without Russia. Ukraine without war. Ukraine with Europe. And Ukraine and Europe without the threat from Moscow. From an address on Ukrainian Statehood Day – the Day of the Baptism of Kyivan Rus–Ukraine. (4/5)

Glad to welcome European Commission President @vonderleyen to Kyiv. Today, on Ukrainian Statehood Day, we are quite rightly devoting considerable attention to our relations with the European Union. We are working to open clusters in Ukraine’s EU accession negotiations. We are now really looking forward to the next European sanctions package – the 21st package – taking effect. We have done a lot to integrate our defense capabilities and production with those of Europe. Today, we also have the first document laying the groundwork for the Drone Deal – a major defense agreement between Ukraine and the European Union. This is a truly historic step in our relations. Going forward, we expect financial support from the EU for our Anti-Ballistic Missile Program. We also discussed today what Ukraine and the European Union need to do together to prepare for winter – energy remains a constant priority. Thank you, Ursula! Thank you to all our friends in Europe!

July 15. Ukraine marks Ukrainian Statehood Day. We are proud to be the heirs of Kyivan Rus and the Cossack state, and to have a thousand years of experience in building our state. A thousand years of courage, of the desire to live in freedom, to live as a sovereign and independent nation, to follow only our own path, and to be who we are – to be Ukraine. We are proud of what everyone has witnessed throughout the full-scale war – Ukraine’s thousand-year-old character. Ukraine respects its history. It remembers every joy and every pain our people have had to endure. But we do not live constantly looking back. We are not focused solely on the history that has already been written. We are shaping Ukraine’s history yet to come. A state that will succeed. An independence that will be guaranteed. From an address on Ukrainian Statehood Day – the Day of the Baptism of Kyivan Rus–Ukraine. (1/5)

items.title: We are building a state that has enough strength and self-respect to do both at once: preserve its heritage and pursue ambitious plans. A Ukraine that values its past and believes in its own future. A state that is not afraid of the phrase “for the first time” – and that, for the first time, is doing what until recently could only have been a dream. For the first time, Ukraine and Europe will build an anti-ballistic shield together. A historic project in which Ukraine is not an observer, but one of the leaders – bringing together countries and Europe’s strongest defense companies. The FREYJA project has been launched. We will do everything necessary to build Europe’s anti-ballistic system by integrating all European anti-ballistic capabilities. So that the skies over our nations are protected. So that dictators cannot dictate how free people in Europe should live. So that the lives of people in Ukraine and in every European country do not depend on whether Putin and those like him have ballistic missiles or not. From an address on Ukrainian Statehood Day – the Day of the Baptism of Kyivan Rus–Ukraine. (2/5)
